gaps.question: Can UnisonoVoiceAmount, UnisonoDetune, and UnisonoSpread be updated in real-time for held notes?
gaps.answer: No. These parameters are read at note-on time only. Changing them while notes are held has no effect on those voices. To update in real-time, use the Detune Modulation or Spread Modulation chains instead of the parameters directly. For voice count changes, all voices must be killed and retriggered.

gaps.question: What happens to a child synth's pitch modulation chain inside a SynthGroup?
gaps.answer: It is bypassed and hidden in the UI. The SynthGroup's shared Pitch Modulation chain is the only pitch modulation path for all children. Independent per-child pitch modulation is not possible.

gaps.question: Why do unison voices click on note-on?
gaps.answer: Each unison voice is given a random start offset (up to ~10ms at 44.1kHz) to prevent phase cancellation. This offset causes an audible click when no amplitude envelope is present. Adding a ~5ms attack time on the group's gain envelope eliminates the click.

gaps.question: How to set independent gain per child without zipper noise?
gaps.answer: Changing a child module's Gain parameter directly causes zipper noise when modulated rapidly. Use a gain modulator (which operates in the 0-1 range, not dB) on the child for smooth, artifact-free gain changes.

gaps.question: How to achieve monophonic behaviour with unison voices?
gaps.answer: SynthGroup has no built-in mono mode. Add the built-in 'Legato with Retrigger' MIDI script to the SynthGroup for monophonic unison.

gaps.question: Does core.pitch_mod inside a scriptnode child observe SynthGroup unison detune?
gaps.answer: No. core.pitch_mod reads pitch modulation values before the SynthGroup applies per-voice unison detune amounts. The detune offset is not visible to scriptnode nodes. Use the clone container instead for fully scriptnode-based unison with per-voice detune.

gaps.question: Is the automatic random start offset sufficient for sampler-based unison?
gaps.answer: The auto random start offset may not be enough to avoid phasing artifacts with sampler children. Manually randomize sample start position in the sampler settings for better results.

gaps.question: Can children inside a SynthGroup have independent MIDI processors?
gaps.answer: Needs documentation. Forum topic suggests this is a common question but details are unclear from the available posts.
